title: ABS Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The ABS( ) function allows you to return the absolute value of a numeric expression.

## Syntax

ABS(numeric_expr)

## Metasymbol

numeric_expr
An expression that is an integer or real number.

## Examples

In this example, if Y = -10, then X = 10.

X = ABS(Y)

title: BNOT Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
BNOT () returns an integer expression that is the result of each bit in the base-2 number of the integer being reversed. This means that the BNOT( ) function changes all one bits to zeros, and zero bits to ones. Additionally, BNOT( ) changes the sign bit. If the value of the sign bit is zero, the integer is positive. If the sign bit value is one, the integer is negative.

## Syntax

BNOT(integer)

title: CALL COMPILE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The CALL COMPILE command includes the input file and echoes out errors and output to the trace file.

Lines in the input file exceeding 132 characters are truncated to 132 characters by the compile. The input file is not modified but only the first 132 characters of the line are compiled.

title: CALL DCL Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: The CALL DCL command enables you to execute a back-end operating system command. CALL DCL spawns an operating system subprocess. The command is executed in that subprocess. title: CALL ECHO Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: The CALL ECHO command echoes an expression to the displayer, log file, or listing file. The CURECHO variable is used to compare to the echo_level. # CCL Functions Listed by Release

List of some helpful CCL functions while doing your MPage coding. They will be listed by Discern Explorer CCL versions. In each of the sections you will be able to find the minimum code level to obtain the CCL version.

## 8.5.6 CCL Functions

body_preview: By default, the CCLUSERDIR directory is used for all files. This includes run-time server logs, temporary sort files, session history files, and any data files created by programs that are not specified to write to another location. Log files and temporary files that are created and used by Discern Explorer programs also are stored in the CCLUSERDIR directory.

title: CEIL Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki

The CEIL( ) function allows you to return the largest integer of a real number. This is a rounding function. The real_expr is rounded up to the next highest whole number.

## Syntax

CEIL(real_expr)

## Metasymbol

_real_expr_
An expression that is a real number.

## Examples

In this example, if X = 5.7, then Y = 6.

Y = CEIL(X)

# Overview

This Reference Page outlines Oracle's stance on Oracle® Advanced Compression.

The Advanced Compression option compresses data by eliminating duplicate values in a single database block. Compressed data stored in a database block is self-contained. That is, all the information needed to re-create the uncompressed data in a block is available within that block. Duplicate values in a block are stored once a...

Windows kernel stack limitations have been experienced when running 32-bit Applications on 64-bit operating systems. Window controls may not be created or resized properly when running on a 64-bit OS such as Windows Server 2008 R2 due to the process exhausting kernel thread stack space. Kernel stack exhaustion has occurred more often when products outside of Cerner Millennium leverage windows...

title: CNVTAGE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki

CNVTAGE( ) calculates an age in years, months, days, or hours, and returns a 12-character string based on the following formula:

IF (AGE < 1 DAY) "HH:MM HOURS"
ELSEIF (AGE < 1 WEEK) "999 DAYS"
ELSEIF (AGE < 1 MONTH) "999 WEEKS"
ELSEIF (AGE < 2 YEAR) "999 MONTHS"
ELSE "999 YEARS"
ENDIF

The default break point for days, weeks, and months are:
Days 7
Weeks 5
Months 24

This is a

title: CREATE TABLE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki

title: CUBE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The CUBE() function is valid only in the Group By clause. When the CUBE function is used in the Group By clause, the query returns subtotals for all combinations of the specified Select expressions. Beginning in Discern Explorer version 8.12.3, the CUBE function must be declared before it is called.

## Syntax

CUBE(select_expression{,select_expression})

## Metasymbols

_select_expression_
A field from the selection...

title: CUME_DIST Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: The CUME DIST( ) function returns the cumulative distribution in the range of 0 to 1 inclusively of a value in a group of rows. CUME_DIST( ) can be used in the select list as an OLAP expression using the OVER( ) clause if you are using Discern Explorer version 8.8.0 or higher and Oracle 10g or higher. CUME_DIST( ) requires an ORDER BY in the OVER( ) clause. Discern Explorer version 8.9.2 introduced support for the WI...

title: CURCODECOVER Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The CURCODECOVER( ) function displays the code coverage information from within a program. It is required that the program be compiled in debug mode and that the COST and CODECOVER traces be turned on. The code coverage information is useful for determining the lines in a program that were executed when testing the program. Lines that were executed were covered by the test.

The code coverage information is displayed...

access_mode | file_org | database_name | file_name
access_mode - 'R' for read or 'W' for write access mode (1 character)
file_org - "I" for indexed or "S" for sequential file organization (1 character)
database_name - database name used in the define command (12 characters)

title: CURLOB Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
CURLOB allows you to pass a BLOB or CLOB parameter to a PLSQL function. It returns the expression but if it is used as bind parameter to PLSQL function, it binds the expression based on the mode.

CURLOB was introduced in version 8.10.0 of Discern Explorer.

## Syntax

CURLOB(expression, mode)

## Metasymbol

_expression_
A BLOB or CLOB expression.

_mode_

Mode| Description
---|---
1| Pass as char
2| Pass as raw
3|...

title: CURLOCALE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki

CURLOCALE(locale_string)

CURMEM(mode)

_mode
_

title: CURPROG Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
CURPROG returns the program name for a program level. It returns a blank space if the program level is not in the current program execution path, otherwise, the program, or subprogram name for that level is returned. The program level must be between 1 and the current level.

## Syntax

CURPROG(level)

## Metasymbol

_level_

An integer value representing the program level.

It returns a blank space if the program level is not in the current program execution path; otherwise, the subroutine name for that level is returned. title: Display Qualifier Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki

FLOOR(real_expr)

An expression that is a real number.

Y = FLOOR(X)

title_clean: FORUPDATE Control Option Using Discern Explorer
body_preview:
The **FORUPDATE** control option selects the rows from the table referred to as range with a locked condition. Locked rows cannot be modified by another process or selected with a locked condition by another process. Rows remain in a locked state until a ROLLBACK or COMMIT is executed. If the rows cannot be locked, the select completes without acquiring the lock.

Oracle only allows locks for single table queries. Se...

title: FORUPDATESKIP Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: The FORUPDATESKIP control option selects rows from the table specified as range with a locked condition. Locked rows cannot be modified by another process or selected by another process with a locked condition. Rows remain in a locked state until a ROLLBACK or COMMIT command is executed. Qualifying rows that are locked by another process are skipped. Using the FORUPDATESKIP control option, only the qualifying rows th...

title: FORUPDATEWAIT Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki

If the rows cannot be locked, the select waits until they can be locked to complete. FORUPDATEWAIT is not generally used because it can...

title: FREE DEFINE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The FREE DEFINE command closes a database previously opened using the DEFINE command and deallocates the defined database name. Any internal memory associated with the database also is deallocated. A maximum of 30 databases can be open at one time.

Each database allocated using the DEFINE command corresponds to an entry in a Discern Explorer internal table. The define table holds a maximum of 30 entries. Discern Exp...

title: FREE RANGE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
FREE RANGE deallocates a range that was previously allocated with the RANGE command. A maximum of 30 ranges can be active at one time.

## Syntax

FREE RANGE alias | ALL

## Metasymbol

_alias_
The abbreviated name of a table used in place of the table name.

## Examples

Example 1

free range p

Example 2

free range e

*[ KB]: Kilobyte

title: FREE RECORD Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The FREE RECORD command allows you to free a record structure that was previously defined using the RECORD command.

By default, record structures created using the RECORD command are freed when the program that creates them completes. Generally, no reason exists to specifically free a record structure in programs. Often using FREE RECORD record_name before using RECORD record_name causes problems when programs are e...

title: FREE SELECT Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview: The FREE SELECT command allows you to release memory allocated for a custom table created by the SELECT INTO TABLE command using the WITH ORGANIZATION = WORK or the WITH ORGANIZATION = TEMPRDB Control Option. Using ORGANIZATION = WORK creates a Discern Explorer in-memory table. Using ORGANIZATION = TEMPRDB creates a temporary in-memory RDBMS table. The memory associated with an in-memory table is freed automatically...

title: FREE SET Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The FREE SET command allows you to remove any global variable assignments created using the SET or DECLARE command, and frees a record that was previously defined.

## Syntax

FREE SET variable GO

## Metasymbol

_variable_

A variable is an area of memory that is given a name. Values can then be assigned to that area of memory using the name. For example, you can create a variable called COUNTER, and assign numbers...

title: FREE SUBROUTINE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The FREE SUBROUTINE command frees a subroutine that was previously defined using the DECLARE command.

## Syntax

FREE SUBROUTINE subroutine_name GO

## Metasymbol

_subroutine_name_

The name of the subroutine that was created using the DECLARE command.

## Examples

The following example demonstrates how to use the FREE SUBROUTINE command:

FREE SUBROUTINE SUB1 GO

GetValidEncounters retrieves valid encounters for a patient based on encounter org security and confid level.

**Note:** Does not check if encounter org security is ON/OFF. If it is OFF, then it will return the complete list of encounters for the patient.

There will be a performance impact if a personId is passed in for a patient not already in context.

title: HB Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
Prints a horizontal bar for the specified length starting in the designated location. The horizontal bar character is selected for each device to be as close to a full horizontal line at mid height in the character cell as possible. For a line printer, the horizontal bar is the dash (-) rather than a solid line.

**Syntax**

{HB/n/l}

**
Metasymbols**

title: HOUR Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
Returns the integer hour (HH) of an expression that is in (HHMM) format or a rdb_dt_tm column. An integer from 0 to 23 is returned.

## Syntax

HOUR(time)
HOUR(rdb_dt_tm)

## Metasymbols

_time_
An expression that is in HHMM format.

_rdb_dt_tm_
A column that stores the date and time.

## Examples

In this example, if the current time is 14:30 (2:30 p.m.), H1 is set to 14.

H1 = HOUR(CURTIME)

The HT command skips horizontally to the designated column. Columns between the current location and the designated column are filled with spaces.

**Syntax**

{HT/n/l}

**Metasymbols**

_n_
The number of the column to skip to. (Required)

_l_
The location at which the skip starts. (Default: Current location)

**Example**
This command prints the text "Page: 1'' starting in column 105.

{HT/105}Page: 1

In these columns, the data has been converted to uppercase and stripped of all non-alphanumeric characters. INDEXUNIQUE(column_name[{,column_name}])

title: LINE using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
Skips the specified number of lines. When not embedded within other DIO tokens, lines will always be skipped before any text is printed out.

**Syntax**

{L[INE]/n}

**Metasymbols**
n
The number of lines to be skipped. (Default: 1)

**Example**
The

{HB/9}{LINE}{B}CHEMISTRY{ENDB}{LINE}{HB/9}

command prints the following:

All directives are one-line commands that must begin in the first column of the Discern Explorer command line or source code file.

_module_name.ext_

MONTH(julian_date)
MONTH(rdb_date_time)

## Metasymbols

_julian_date_
An expression that is an Internal System Date.

_rdb_date_time_

title: NOPRESERVE Control Option Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
title_clean: NOPRESERVE Control Option Using Discern Explorer
body_preview:
The NOPRESERVE control option is used with the SELECT INTO TABLE table_name command and the ORGANIZATION = "T" control option to create a temporary Relational Database Management System (RDBMS) table to automatically delete the rows from the table at the end of the transaction. A commit or rollback ends the transaction. When using NOPRESERVE, commit and rollback have the same effect.

* If optimizer mode is RBO, NORDBBIND() is ignored and bind variables are used in the query that is sent to the RDBMS.

title: NULLCHECK( ) Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The NULLCHECK () function returns the expression if **nullind** equals zero, otherwise it returns the default value.

## Syntax

NULLCHECK(expression, default, nullind)

## Metasymbols

_expression_
A value of any data type.

_default_
A value of any data type, but it must be consistent with the expression used.

A condition that is set to 1 if the expression is null or zero if the expression is not null....

December 2024 edited January 2025 in

An OUTERJOIN is a type of join that displays rows from a preceding table, even if information does not exist on subsequent tables that were joined. OUTERJOINs allow you to display a row from the first table, even if the second table does not have any matching rows.

Two options are available when performing an OUTERJOIN at the RDBMS level.

* OUTERJOIN function
* LEFT JOIN in the FROM clause

title: PAGE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
This command prints the current value of the page counter starting at the specified location.

## Syntax

{P[AGE]/l}

## Metasymbol

l
The column location of the left-most character of the page number. (Default: Current location)

## Example

The following example uses the IPC, NP, SKIP, and PAGE commands to print PAGE 1' in column 110 on a new page:

{IPC}{NP}{SKIP/110}Page{PAGE}

Prints text or output on the page at the location specified.

## Syntax

{POS[ITION]/x/y}

An integer representing the specific x-axis location on the page, based on 1/72 inch increments supported on laser printers and printers with a page description language. (Required)

The control option DIO=38 generates a Portable Document Format (PDF) output. Use this option in place of existing reports that use the DIO=08 option, intended for compatibility with Web-based report viewing. Below are the differences between DIO=08 and DIO=38 options.

* Bar codes are displayed at the top of the current line's text in .PDF file unless the bar code is smaller than the height of the text, in which case...

title: PostScript Report Example Using Discern Visual Developer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
title_clean: PostScript Report Example Using Discern Visual Developer
body_preview:
The following example displays the syntax used to create a simple PostScript report:

drop program test_sample2_dvd go
create program test_sample2_dvd
prompt "output device" = MINE
select into $1
testfield = "My PostScript Report Example"
from dummyt
detail
row 0 col 5 testfield
with format,dio=postscript
end go

*[ KB]: Kilobyte
*[235.6k]: 235,595
*[551.6k]: 551,554
*[1.0m]: 1,049,058

title: PostScript Using Visual Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The PostScript option allows you to print your report to a PostScript printer or the displayer. Formatting that you build in your report is recognized and printed or displayed. When postscript output is sent to the displayer, only one page at a time is rendered. To view subsequent pages, use the next page button in the displayer.

Creating an MOEW from workflows that initiated from the MOEW or PowerOrders are not supported. The current architecture was designed around only having one MOEW created at a time. The PRESERVE control option is used with the SELECT INTO TABLE table_name command and ORGANIZATION = "T" control option to create a temporary Relational Database Management System (RDBMS) table to preserve the table rows until the end of the session.

By default, when the CBO is used, the plus zero (+0) in the WHERE clause is not retained in the query that is passed to the Relational Database Management System (RDBMS). When the RDBCBOPLUSZERO control option is used, the +0 is retained in the query that is passed to the RDBMS.

title: Simultaneous Multi-Threading (SMT) and Cerner Millennium - CernerWorks Reference Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
# Overview

AIX version 6.1, in combination with POWER7 hardware, introduced Four-Way Simultaneous Multi-Threading (SMT4), a feature that allows four logical processors per physical core. SMT4 is designed to keep multiple threads processing concurrently, which can increase processor efficiency. SMT4 is available only with POWER7 hardware running AIX 6.1 or higher.

AIX version 7.1, in combination with POWER8 hardware...

title: UPDATE Using Discern Explorer - Discern Help Pages - Cerner Wiki
body_preview:
The UPDATE command updates existing rows in a table. Only authorized Oracle personnel should use the UPDATE command on Oracle production tables.

Your RDBMS sign-on controls which RDBMS tables you can update. To UPDATE rows on a non-RDBMS table, you must issue a DEFINE command with the MODIFY option to allow updates to the file; otherwise, the file is opened as read-only.

body_preview:
Pam_Boere Oracle_Customer **Contributor**

October 2025 in Social Groups

Good evening,

YEAR(julian_date)
YEAR(rdb_date_time)
